art_market.summary: Seoul's art scene in late March 2026 is vibrant with significant exhibitions, including retrospectives and institutional queer art showcases. The market is showing resilience and structural adaptation, with new digital platforms emphasizing transparency.
new_exhibitions: Kim Yun-shin Retrospective (Hoam Museum, Mar 17 - Jun 28)
Spectrosynthesis Seoul (Art Sonje Center, Mar 20 - Jun 28)
Damien Hirst Retrospective (MMCA Seoul, Opened Mar 2026)
ongoing: Jae-Eun Choi : Where Beings Be (Seosomun Main Branch, until Apr 5)
March Avery: Form into Color (Esther Schipper, until Apr 25)

stock_market.summary: As of March 23, 2026, the KOSPI is anticipated to face volatility due to geopolitical tensions and the start of the shareholder meeting season. Analysts project a weekly band of 5,500-6,100 points.

key_indicators.KOSPI_Status: Opening to volatility due to geopolitical tensions (Middle East) and oil price fluctuations.
key_indicators.Market_Sentiment: Cautiously resilient; recovering from recent pressure.
key_indicators.Key_Drivers: Semiconductor rebound (Samsung, SK Hynix), start of annual shareholder meeting season, potential U.S. Federal Reserve policy shifts.
key_indicators.Outlook: Focus on shareholder returns and Q1 earnings expectations amidst 5,500-6,100 point band.
